author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #585. That issue added the trait surface (cdf, icdf, mean, mode, variance, stddev) with default impls returning \`InvalidArgument\` for unsupported distributions, and implemented the properties on 10 distributions: Normal, Uniform, Exponential, Laplace, Cauchy, Bernoulli, Beta, Gamma, Poisson, LogNormal, HalfNormal, Gumbel.

## Remaining 17 distributions
Still need implementations on:
- StudentT (mean = loc if df>1 else NaN; var = scale²·df/(df-2) if df>2)
- Weibull (mean = scale·Γ(1+1/concentration); var = scale²·[Γ(1+2/c) − Γ(1+1/c)²]; mode requires concentration > 1)
- Pareto (mean = αβ/(α-1) if α>1; var = αβ²/((α-1)²(α-2)) if α>2; mode = β)
- Kumaraswamy (mean = b·B(1+1/a, b); cdf and icdf are closed-form)
- VonMises (no closed-form cdf; mean = mu; mode = mu; variance via Bessel functions)
- Categorical / Multinomial / OneHotCategorical (mean = probs; mode = argmax)
- Dirichlet (mean = α / Σα; mode if all α > 1; variance per-component)
- MultivariateNormal / LowRankMultivariateNormal (mean = loc; covariance handled via cov_factor + cov_diag)
- MixtureSameFamily (mean = Σ wᵢ μᵢ if components have mean)
- Independent (delegate to base distribution; reshape last dims)
- RelaxedBernoulli, RelaxedOneHotCategorical (Concrete relaxation; usually no closed-form CDF for non-degenerate temperatures)

## Hard cases — would need new infra
- Beta cdf/icdf require regularized incomplete beta function (no current Rust dep)
- Gamma cdf/icdf require regularized incomplete gamma (same)
- StudentT cdf/icdf require regularized incomplete beta
- VonMises cdf via numerical integration (sum over Bessel function ratios)
- Multinomial / Dirichlet cdf are not univariate — convention is to return per-component CDFs

These can land incrementally; the trait already accepts \`InvalidArgument\` as a sentinel, so partial implementations don't break anything.
